stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of Llama and Facebook tags indicates relevance to major large language model (LLM) developments.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags, including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the ongoing advancements in large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: SpaceX gets $4 billion contract to build missile-tracking ‘Golden Dome’ satellites
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: SpaceX secured a $4.16 billion contract from the Pentagon.
The contract is for building missile-tracking satellites for the 'Golden Dome' defense system.
These new sensor-equipped satellites will enable space-based detection and tracking of targets.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, a novel LLM capable of generating editable PCB schematics directly from natural language requests.
Addresses a significant bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
Overcomes key challenges in the field, specifically the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this task.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels.
The dataset is designed for visual generative modeling and includes 1M test examples, captioned by a state-of-the-art VLM.
Crucially, all images are permissively licensed for both research and commercial use, addressing data accessibility issues.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are all teasing Nvidia’s new N1X laptop processors
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Nvidia is reportedly preparing to announce its own Arm-powered laptop processors at Computex.
The announcement is being teased by Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm, signaling industry anticipation.
This development points toward a potential shift in the PC landscape with integrated, specialized AI/GPU capabilities.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the critical lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing and analysis of LLM behaviors and failure modes based on their underlying data mixture.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the compositional residual eps*, to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and predefined cross-component constraints.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to analyze the hidden pretraining data composition of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research addresses the lack of transparency regarding LLM 'digital DNA' (training data mixture), which dictates model behavior.
The tool estimates the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only the text generated by the target model.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses the current manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic design, a significant bottleneck in hardware development.
Overcomes key challenges in the field, specifically the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this task.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) in Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a novel method designed to make TTFT practical by improving efficiency.
The core technical contribution involves using 'Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching' to balance the trade-off between speed and model quality during adaptation.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals before resource expenditure.
The benchmark is curated from ICLR submissions, providing a real-world test set for evaluating AI scientific judgment.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the critical lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing of LLM behavior and failure modes by analyzing their 'digital DNA' (training data mixture).

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language descriptions.
The research addresses a significant gap in hardware design, as PCB schematic creation is currently a manual and expertise-intensive process.
The core technical challenge addressed is developing an LLM-suitable representation and acquiring a large-scale dataset for this domain.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global consistency.
Introduces a formal metric, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and defined cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The context suggests ongoing community interest in large language models (LLMs) and their evaluation.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these AI agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: AI training startup Shift is offering free home cleaning services in exchange for footage.
The service aims to gather real-world data by filming users performing daily chores.
Shift plans to expand its data collection and cleaning services to other major cities like London.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
This represents a significant cybersecurity takedown operation.

stories.title: Startup offers free home cleaning—if it can record it all for robot training
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: A startup is offering free home cleaning services in exchange for human participants wearing head cameras.
The collected data is intended to train autonomous robots, highlighting the need for real-world human interaction data.
This exemplifies the growing trend of compensating humans for providing data for AI/ML development, specifically in robotics.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude models) when supervised by a physicist to develop scientific software (CLAX-PT).
It provides a quantified case study detailing 15 supervision events, classifying the level of human intervention required.
The AI demonstrated autonomous problem-solving capabilities, resolving ten issues through iterative testing, supplemented by expert domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: VideoMLA introduces Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) to video diffusion models.
It addresses memory and latency issues by replacing per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent KV cache.
The work builds upon existing fixed-size sliding-window KV cache approaches in long-rollout causal video diffusion.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights a key research gap: existing benchmarks lack natural-language rationales for anomaly detection, hindering VLM interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to enable VLMs to produce grounded and interpretable anomaly detection decisions.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, which relies on externalizing intermediate thoughts (autoregressive generation).
The proposed method aims to mimic human cognition by utilizing internal working memory for information manipulation during reasoning.
This approach seeks to decouple internal computation from the external token generation process to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base model, can elicit comparable reasoning to traditional reinforcement learning fine-tuning.
This method bypasses the need for extensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary remaining challenge for practical application is developing an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies after static visual encoding.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) where fixed aggregation weights cause bias due to unequal client contributions.
Proposes the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to improve fairness and stability in distributed FL systems.
Focuses on enhancing FL's ability to handle heterogeneous and privacy-sensitive data by accurately weighting client contributions over time.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method proposes achieving comparable reasoning in frontier models by sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model, bypassing traditional reinforcement learning fine-tuning.
This approach offers a potential shortcut by eliminating the need for additional training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The practical implementation challenge lies in efficiently developing a sampler capable of accurately 'mixing' to this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in RLHF: the reliance on static reward models that struggle with diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to adapt to unseen or heterogeneous human preference domains.
This aims to overcome the rigidity of existing multi-reward frameworks that are limited to predefined preference sets.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM reasoning by decoupling internal computation from autoregressive token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition, utilizing 'working memory' to manipulate information internally without externalizing intermediate steps.
The goal is to significantly improve the latent reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: The research introduces 'COMPOSE' for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses a gap by simultaneously considering both the direction of prior work (grounding) and formal structural dependencies.
This represents an advancement in grounded future mathematical generation, moving beyond models that only consider one constraint.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base language model, can achieve comparable reasoning performance to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This method potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training steps like reinforcement learning, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical hurdle for practical application is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately drawing samples from this derived power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses issues in current LLM leaderboards where pairwise rankings are often unresolved under standard evaluation designs.
Specific examples show that 11 of 40 Open LLM Leaderboard v1 comparisons and 4 of 9 MMLU-Pro pairs fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
The research proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to better diagnose ranking discrepancies.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation by creating a text-to-FHIR dataset for clinical reasoning.
Focuses on generating clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text, mirroring EHR data.
Aims to enable controllable benchmarking of diagnostic reasoning in real-world clinical settings.

stories.title: Perry Compiles TypeScript directly to executables using SWC and LLVM
stories.author: 0x1997
bullet_points: Perry has developed a method to compile TypeScript directly into native executables.
The compilation process leverages SWC and LLVM.
This represents a significant optimization/tooling advancement for TypeScript development.

stories.title: How Model Distillation Actually Works (and What the 'China Distilled Our Model' Headlines Really Mean)
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: Provides a practical, non-hype explanation of the mechanics of knowledge distillation in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the nuances of distilling knowledge from closed APIs, differentiating it from standard training.
Analyzes the context and implications behind recent industry claims regarding model distillation (e.g., OpenAI/Anthropic vs. DeepSeek).

stories.title: Building a Verification-First AI Coding Agent: Why I Abandoned "Generate-and-Pray"
stories.author: manuelito_ash
bullet_points: Addresses a critical flaw in current AI coding assistants: the 'generate-and-pray' approach.
Introduces a novel solution using a Go engine to enforce 9 deterministic verification gates.
Focuses on building a 'verification-first' AI coding agent, signaling a shift towards reliability in AI-generated code.

stories.title: Harness Engineering: The Code Around the Model Is the Hard Part
stories.author: turacthethinker
bullet_points: The article highlights the often-overlooked complexity of the 'harness' surrounding AI models.
It argues that the infrastructure (loops, tool dispatch) is as critical as the model's performance.
This suggests a shift in focus from pure model benchmarking to end-to-end system engineering in AI deployment.
